verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचकस्य हेतो: परित्यक्ता आचार्येणात्मन: सुरा: ।एतदाचक्ष्व भगवञ्छिष्याणामक्रमं गुरौ ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācakasya hetoḥ parityaktāācāryeṇātmanaḥ surāḥetad ācakṣva bhagavañchiṣyāṇām akramaṁ gurau
verses.text: Mahārāja Parīkṣit inquired from Śukadeva Gosvāmī: O great sage, why did the spiritual master of the demigods, Bṛhaspati, reject the demigods, who were his own disciples? What offense did the demigods commit against their spiritual master? Please describe to me this incident.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचइन्द्रस्त्रिभुवनैश्वर्यमदोल्लङ्घितसत्पथ: ।मरुद्भ‍िर्वसुभी रुद्रैरादित्यैर्ऋभुभिर्नृप ॥ २ ॥विश्वेदेवैश्च साध्यैश्च नासत्याभ्यां परिश्रित: ।सिद्धचारणगन्धर्वैर्मुनिभिर्ब्रह्मवादिभि: ॥ ३ ॥विद्याधराप्सरोभिश्च किन्नरै: पतगोरगै: ।निषेव्यमाणो मघवान्स्तूयमानश्च भारत ॥ ४ ॥उपगीयमानो ललितमास्थानाध्यासनाश्रित: ।पाण्डुरेणातपत्रेण चन्द्रमण्डलचारुणा ॥ ५ ॥युक्तश्चान्यै: पारमेष्ठ्यैश्चामरव्यजनादिभि: ।विराजमान: पौलम्या सहार्धासनया भृशम् ॥ ६ ॥स यदा परमाचार्यं देवानामात्मनश्च ह ।नाभ्यनन्दत सम्प्राप्तं प्रत्युत्थानासनादिभि: ॥ ७ ॥वाचस्पतिं मुनिवरं सुरासुरनमस्कृतम् ।नोच्चचालासनादिन्द्र: पश्यन्नपि सभागतम् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācaindras tribhuvanaiśvarya-madollaṅghita-satpathaḥmarudbhir vasubhī rudrairādityair ṛbhubhir nṛpa
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King, once upon a time, the King of heaven, Indra, being extremely proud because of his great opulence of the three worlds, transgressed the law of Vedic etiquette. Seated on his throne, he was surrounded by the Maruts, Vasus, Rudras, Ādityas, Ṛbhus, Viśvadevas, Sādhyas, Aśvinī-kumāras, Siddhas, Cāraṇas and Gandharvas and by great saintly persons. Also surrounding him were the Vidyādharas, Apsarās, Kinnaras, Patagas [birds] and Uragas [snakes]. All of them were offering Indra their respects and services, and the Apsarās and Gandharvas were dancing and singing with very sweet musical instruments. Over Indra’s head was a white umbrella as effulgent as the full moon. Fanned by yak-tail whisks and served with all the paraphernalia of a great king, Indra was sitting with his wife, Śacīdevī, who occupied half the throne, when the great sage Bṛhaspati appeared in that assembly. Bṛhaspati, the best of the sages, was the spiritual master of Indra and the demigods and was respected by the demigods and demons alike. Nevertheless, although Indra saw his spiritual master before him, he did not rise from his own seat or offer a seat to his spiritual master, nor did Indra offer him a respectful welcome. Indra did nothing to show him respect.

verses.sanskrit: ततो निर्गत्य सहसा कविराङ्गिरस: प्रभु: ।आययौ स्वगृहं तूष्णीं विद्वान्श्रीमदविक्रियाम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato nirgatya sahasākavir āṅgirasaḥ prabhuḥāyayau sva-gṛhaṁ tūṣṇīṁvidvān śrī-mada-vikriyām
verses.text: Bṛhaspati knew everything that would happen in the future. Seeing Indra’s transgression of etiquette, he completely understood that Indra was puffed up by his material opulence. Although able to curse Indra, he did not do so. Instead, he left the assembly and in silence returned to his home.

verses.sanskrit: तर्ह्येव प्रतिबुध्येन्द्रो गुरुहेलनमात्मन: ।गर्हयामास सदसि स्वयमात्मानमात्मना ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tarhy eva pratibudhyendroguru-helanam ātmanaḥgarhayām āsa sadasisvayam ātmānam ātmanā
verses.text: Indra, the King of heaven, could immediately understand his mistake. Realizing he had disrespected his spiritual master, he condemned himself in the presence of all the members of the assembly.

verses.sanskrit: अहो बत मयासाधु कृतं वै दभ्रबुद्धिना ।यन्मयैश्वर्यमत्तेन गुरु: सदसि कात्कृत: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho bata mayāsādhukṛtaṁ vai dabhra-buddhināyan mayaiśvarya-mattenaguruḥ sadasi kātkṛtaḥ
verses.text: Alas, what a regrettable deed I have committed because of my lack of intelligence and my pride in my material opulences. I failed to show respect to my spiritual master when he entered this assembly, and thus I have insulted him.

verses.sanskrit: को गृध्येत्पण्डितो लक्ष्मीं त्रिपिष्टपपतेरपि ।ययाहमासुरं भावं नीतोऽद्य विबुधेश्वर: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ko gṛdhyet paṇḍito lakṣmīṁtripiṣṭapa-pater apiyayāham āsuraṁ bhāvaṁnīto ’dya vibudheśvaraḥ
verses.text: Although I am King of the demigods, who are situated in the mode of goodness, I was proud of a little opulence and polluted by false ego. Under the circumstances, who in this world would accept such riches at the risk of falling down? Alas! I condemn my wealth and opulence.

verses.sanskrit: य: पारमेष्ठ्यं धिषणमधितिष्ठन्न कञ्चन ।प्रत्युत्तिष्ठेदिति ब्रूयुर्धर्मं ते न परं विदु: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yaḥ pārameṣṭhyaṁ dhiṣaṇamadhitiṣṭhan na kañcanapratyuttiṣṭhed iti brūyurdharmaṁ te na paraṁ viduḥ
verses.text: If a person says, “One who is situated on the exalted throne of a king should not stand up to show respect to another king or a brāhmaṇa,” it is to be understood that he does not know the superior religious principles.

verses.sanskrit: तेषां कुपथदेष्टृणां पततां तमसि ह्यध: ।ये श्रद्दध्युर्वचस्ते वै मज्जन्त्यश्मप्लवा इव ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: teṣāṁ kupatha-deṣṭṝṇāṁpatatāṁ tamasi hy adhaḥye śraddadhyur vacas te vaimajjanty aśma-plavā iva
verses.text: Leaders who have fallen into ignorance and who mislead people by directing them to the path of destruction [as described in the previous verse] are, in effect, boarding a stone boat, and so too are those who blindly follow them. A stone boat would be unable to float and would sink in the water with its passengers. Similarly, those who mislead people go to hell, and their followers go with them.

verses.sanskrit: अथाहममराचार्यमगाधधिषणं द्विजम् ।प्रसादयिष्ये निशठ: शीर्ष्णा तच्चरणं स्पृशन् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāham amarācāryamagādha-dhiṣaṇaṁ dvijamprasādayiṣye niśaṭhaḥśīrṣṇā tac-caraṇaṁ spṛśan
verses.text: King Indra said: Therefore with great frankness and without duplicity I shall now bow my head at the lotus feet of Bṛhaspati, the spiritual master of the demigods. Because he is in the mode of goodness, he is fully aware of all knowledge and is the best of the brāhmaṇas. Now I shall touch his lotus feet and offer my obeisances unto him to try to satisfy him.

verses.sanskrit: एवं चिन्तयतस्तस्य मघोनो भगवान् गृहात् ।बृहस्पतिर्गतोऽद‍ृष्टां गतिमध्यात्ममायया ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ cintayatas tasyamaghono bhagavān gṛhātbṛhaspatir gato ’dṛṣṭāṁgatim adhyātma-māyayā
verses.text: While Indra, the King of the demigods, thought in this way and repented in his own assembly, Bṛhaspati, the most powerful spiritual master, understood his mind. Thus he became invisible to Indra and left home, for Bṛhaspati was spiritually more powerful than King Indra.

verses.sanskrit: गुरोर्नाधिगत: संज्ञां परीक्षन् भगवान् स्वराट् ।ध्यायन् धिया सुरैर्युक्त: शर्म नालभतात्मन: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: guror nādhigataḥ saṁjñāṁparīkṣan bhagavān svarāṭdhyāyan dhiyā surair yuktaḥśarma nālabhatātmanaḥ
verses.text: Although Indra searched vigorously with the assistance of the other demigods, he could not find Bṛhaspati. Then Indra thought, “Alas, my spiritual master has become dissatisfied with me, and now I have no means of achieving good fortune.” Although Indra was surrounded by demigods, he could not find peace of mind.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वैवासुरा: सर्व आश्रित्यौशनसं मतम् ।देवान् प्रत्युद्यमं चक्रुर्दुर्मदा आततायिन: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tac chrutvaivāsurāḥ sarvaāśrityauśanasaṁ matamdevān pratyudyamaṁ cakrurdurmadā ātatāyinaḥ
verses.text: Hearing of the pitiable condition of King Indra, the demons, following the instructions of their guru, Śukrācārya, equipped themselves with weapons and declared war against the demigods.

verses.sanskrit: तैर्विसृष्टेषुभिस्तीक्ष्णैर्निर्भिन्नाङ्गोरुबाहव: ।ब्रह्माणं शरणं जग्मु: सहेन्द्रा नतकन्धरा: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tair visṛṣṭeṣubhis tīkṣṇairnirbhinnāṅgoru-bāhavaḥbrahmāṇaṁ śaraṇaṁ jagmuḥsahendrā nata-kandharāḥ
verses.text: The demigods’ heads, thighs and arms and the other parts of their bodies were injured by the sharp arrows of the demons. The demigods, headed by Indra, saw no other course than to immediately approach Lord Brahmā with bowed heads for shelter and proper instruction.

verses.sanskrit: तांस्तथाभ्यर्दितान्वीक्ष्य भगवानात्मभूरज: ।कृपया परया देव उवाच परिसान्त्वयन् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁs tathābhyarditān vīkṣyabhagavān ātmabhūr ajaḥkṛpayā parayā devauvāca parisāntvayan
verses.text: When the most powerful Lord Brahmā saw the demigods coming toward him, their bodies gravely injured by the arrows of the demons, he pacified them by his great causeless mercy and spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीब्रह्मोवाचअहो बत सुरश्रेष्ठा ह्यभद्रं व: कृतं महत् ।ब्रह्मिष्ठं ब्राह्मणं दान्तमैश्वर्यान्नाभ्यनन्दत ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-brahmovācaaho bata sura-śreṣṭhāhy abhadraṁ vaḥ kṛtaṁ mahatbrahmiṣṭhaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ dāntamaiśvaryān nābhyanandata
verses.text: Lord Brahmā said: O best of the demigods, unfortunately, because of madness resulting from your material opulence, you failed to receive Bṛhaspati properly when he came to your assembly. Because he is aware of the Supreme Brahman and fully in control of his senses, he is the best of the brāhmaṇas. Therefore it is very astonishing that you have acted impudently toward him.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यायमनयस्यासीत् परेभ्यो व: पराभव: ।प्रक्षीणेभ्य: स्ववैरिभ्य: समृद्धानां च यत्सुरा: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyāyam anayasyāsītparebhyo vaḥ parābhavaḥprakṣīṇebhyaḥ sva-vairibhyaḥsamṛddhānāṁ ca yat surāḥ
verses.text: Because of your misbehavior toward Bṛhaspati, you have been defeated by the demons. My dear demigods, since the demons were weak, having been defeated by you several times, how else could you, who were so advanced in opulence, be defeated by them?

verses.sanskrit: मघवन् द्विषत: पश्य प्रक्षीणान् गुर्वतिक्रमात् ।सम्प्रत्युपचितान् भूय: काव्यमाराध्य भक्तित: ।आददीरन्निलयनं ममापि भृगुदेवता: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: maghavan dviṣataḥ paśyaprakṣīṇān gurv-atikramātsampraty upacitān bhūyaḥkāvyam ārādhya bhaktitaḥādadīran nilayanaṁmamāpi bhṛgu-devatāḥ
verses.text: O Indra, your enemies, the demons, were extremely weak because of their disrespect toward Śukrācārya, but since they have now worshiped Śukrācārya with great devotion, they have again become powerful. By their devotion to Śukrācārya, they have increased their strength so much that now they are even able to easily seize my abode from me.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिपिष्टपं किं गणयन्त्यभेद्य-मन्त्रा भृगूणामनुशिक्षितार्था: ।न विप्रगोविन्दगवीश्वराणांभवन्त्यभद्राणि नरेश्वराणाम् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tripiṣṭapaṁ kiṁ gaṇayanty abhedya-mantrā bhṛgūṇām anuśikṣitārthāḥna vipra-govinda-gav-īśvarāṇāṁbhavanty abhadrāṇi nareśvarāṇām
verses.text: Because of their firm determination to follow the instructions of Śukrācārya, his disciples, the demons, are now unconcerned about the demigods. In fact, kings or others who have determined faith in the mercy of brāhmaṇas, cows and the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa, and who always worship these three are always strong in their position.

verses.sanskrit: तद्विश्वरूपं भजताशु विप्रंतपस्विनं त्वाष्ट्रमथात्मवन्तम् ।सभाजितोऽर्थान् स विधास्यते वोयदि क्षमिष्यध्वमुतास्य कर्म ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad viśvarūpaṁ bhajatāśu vipraṁtapasvinaṁ tvāṣṭram athātmavantamsabhājito ’rthān sa vidhāsyate voyadi kṣamiṣyadhvam utāsya karma
verses.text: O demigods, I instruct you to approach Viśvarūpa, the son of Tvaṣṭā, and accept him as your guru. He is a pure and very powerful brāhmaṇa undergoing austerity and penances. Pleased by your worship, he will fulfill your desires, provided that you tolerate his being inclined to side with the demons.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचत एवमुदिता राजन् ब्रह्मणा विगतज्वरा: ।ऋषिं त्वाष्ट्रमुपव्रज्य परिष्वज्येदमब्रुवन् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācata evam uditā rājanbrahmaṇā vigata-jvarāḥṛṣiṁ tvāṣṭram upavrajyapariṣvajyedam abruvan
verses.text: Śrīla Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: Thus advised by Lord Brahmā and relieved of their anxiety, all the demigods went to the sage Viśvarūpa, the son of Tvaṣṭā. My dear King, they embraced him and spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीदेवा ऊचु:वयं तेऽतिथय: प्राप्ता आश्रमं भद्रमस्तु ते ।काम: सम्पाद्यतां तात पितृणां समयोचित: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-devā ūcuḥvayaṁ te ’tithayaḥ prāptāāśramaṁ bhadram astu tekāmaḥ sampādyatāṁ tātapitṝṇāṁ samayocitaḥ
verses.text: The demigods said: Beloved Viśvarūpa, may there be all good fortune for you. We, the demigods, have come to your āśrama as your guests. Please try to fulfill our desires according to the time, since we are on the level of your parents.

verses.sanskrit: पुत्राणां हि परो धर्म: पितृशुश्रूषणं सताम् ।अपि पुत्रवतां ब्रह्मन् किमुत ब्रह्मचारिणाम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: putrāṇāṁ hi paro dharmaḥpitṛ-śuśrūṣaṇaṁ satāmapi putravatāṁ brahmankim uta brahmacāriṇām
verses.text: O brāhmaṇa, the highest duty of a son, even though he has sons of his own, is to serve his parents, and what to speak of a son who is a brahmacārī?

verses.sanskrit: आचार्यो ब्रह्मणो मूर्ति: पिता मूर्ति: प्रजापते: ।भ्राता मरुत्पतेर्मूर्तिर्माता साक्षात् क्षितेस्तनु: ॥ २९ ॥दयाया भगिनी मूर्तिर्धर्मस्यात्मातिथि: स्वयम् ।अग्नेरभ्यागतो मूर्ति: सर्वभूतानि चात्मन: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: ācāryo brahmaṇo mūrtiḥpitā mūrtiḥ prajāpateḥbhrātā marutpater mūrtirmātā sākṣāt kṣites tanuḥ
verses.text: The ācārya, the spiritual master who teaches all the Vedic knowledge and gives initiation by offering the sacred thread, is the personification of all the Vedas. Similarly, a father personifies Lord Brahmā; a brother, King Indra; a mother, the planet earth; and a sister, mercy. A guest personifies religious principles, an invited guest personifies the demigod Agni, and all living entities personify Lord Viṣṇu, the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् पितृणामार्तानामार्तिं परपराभवम् ।तपसापनयंस्तात सन्देशं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmāt pitṝṇām ārtānāmārtiṁ para-parābhavamtapasāpanayaṁs tātasandeśaṁ kartum arhasi
verses.text: Dear son, we have been defeated by our enemies, and therefore we are very much aggrieved. Please mercifully fulfill our desires by relieving our distress through the strength of your austerities. Please fulfill our prayers.

verses.sanskrit: वृणीमहे त्वोपाध्यायं ब्रह्मिष्ठं ब्राह्मणं गुरुम् ।यथाञ्जसा विजेष्याम: सपत्नांस्तव तेजसा ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛṇīmahe tvopādhyāyaṁbrahmiṣṭhaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ gurumyathāñjasā vijeṣyāmaḥsapatnāṁs tava tejasā
verses.text: Since you are completely aware of the Supreme Brahman, you are a perfect brāhmaṇa, and therefore you are the spiritual master of all orders of life. We accept you as our spiritual master and director so that by the power of your austerity we may easily defeat the enemies who have conquered us.

verses.sanskrit: न गर्हयन्ति ह्यर्थेषु यविष्ठाङ्‌घ्य्रभिवादनम् ।छन्दोभ्योऽन्यत्र न ब्रह्मन् वयो ज्यैष्ठ्यस्य कारणम् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: na garhayanti hy artheṣuyaviṣṭhāṅghry-abhivādanamchandobhyo ’nyatra na brahmanvayo jyaiṣṭhyasya kāraṇam
verses.text: The demigods continued: Do not fear criticism for being younger than us. Such etiquette does not apply in regard to Vedic mantras. Except in relationship to Vedic mantras, seniority is determined by age, but one may offer respectful obeisances even to a younger person who is advanced in chanting Vedic mantras. Therefore although you are junior in relationship to us, you may become our priest without hesitation.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीऋषिरुवाचअभ्यर्थित: सुरगणै: पौरहित्ये महातपा: ।स विश्वरूपस्तानाह प्रसन्न: श्लक्ष्णया गिरा ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-ṛṣir uvācaabhyarthitaḥ sura-gaṇaiḥpaurahitye mahā-tapāḥsa viśvarūpas tān āhaprasannaḥ ślakṣṇayā girā
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: When all the demigods requested the great Viśvarūpa to be their priest, Viśvarūpa, who was advanced in austerities, was very pleased. He replied to them as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीविश्‍वरूप उवाचविगर्हितं धर्मशीलैर्ब्रह्मवर्चउपव्ययम् ।कथं नु मद्विधो नाथा लोकेशैरभियाचितम् ।प्रत्याख्यास्यति तच्छिष्य: स एव स्वार्थ उच्यते ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-viśvarūpa uvācavigarhitaṁ dharma-śīlairbrahmavarca-upavyayamkathaṁ nu mad-vidho nāthālokeśair abhiyācitampratyākhyāsyati tac-chiṣyaḥsa eva svārtha ucyate
verses.text: Śrī Viśvarūpa said: O demigods, although the acceptance of priesthood is decried as causing the loss of previously acquired brahminical power, how can someone like me refuse to accept your personal request? You are all exalted commanders of the entire universe. I am your disciple and must take many lessons from you. Therefore I cannot refuse you. I must agree for my own benefit.

verses.sanskrit: अकिञ्चनानां हि धनं शिलोञ्छनंतेनेह निर्वर्तितसाधुसत्क्रिय: ।कथं विगर्ह्यं नु करोम्यधीश्वरा:पौरोधसं हृष्यति येन दुर्मति: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: akiñcanānāṁ hi dhanaṁ śiloñchanaṁteneha nirvartita-sādhu-satkriyaḥkathaṁ vigarhyaṁ nu karomy adhīśvarāḥpaurodhasaṁ hṛṣyati yena durmatiḥ
verses.text: O exalted governors of various planets, the true brāhmaṇa, who has no material possessions, maintains himself by the profession of accepting śiloñchana. In other words, he picks up grains left in the field and on the ground in the wholesale marketplace. By this means, householder brāhmaṇas who actually abide by the principles of austerity and penance maintain themselves and their families and perform all necessary pious activities. A brāhmaṇa who desires to achieve happiness by gaining wealth through professional priesthood must certainly have a very low mind. How shall I accept such priesthood?

verses.sanskrit: तथापि न प्रतिब्रूयां गुरुभि: प्रार्थितं कियत् ।भवतां प्रार्थितं सर्वं प्राणैरर्थैश्च साधये ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāpi na pratibrūyāṁgurubhiḥ prārthitaṁ kiyatbhavatāṁ prārthitaṁ sarvaṁprāṇair arthaiś ca sādhaye
verses.text: All of you are my superiors. Therefore although accepting priesthood is sometimes reproachable, I cannot refuse even a small request from you. I agree to be your priest. I shall fulfill your request by dedicating my life and possessions.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचतेभ्य एवं प्रतिश्रुत्य विश्वरूपो महातपा: ।पौरहित्यं वृतश्चक्रे परमेण समाधिना ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācatebhya evaṁ pratiśrutyaviśvarūpo mahā-tapāḥpaurahityaṁ vṛtaś cakreparameṇa samādhinā
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: O King, after making this promise to the demigods, the exalted Viśvarūpa, surrounded by the demigods, performed the necessary priestly activities with great enthusiasm and attention.

verses.sanskrit: सुरद्विषां श्रियं गुप्तामौशनस्यापि विद्यया ।आच्छिद्यादान्महेन्द्राय वैष्णव्या विद्यया विभु: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sura-dviṣāṁ śriyaṁ guptāmauśanasyāpi vidyayāācchidyādān mahendrāyavaiṣṇavyā vidyayā vibhuḥ
verses.text: The opulence of the demons, who are generally known as the enemies of the demigods, was protected by the talents and tactics of Śukrācārya, but Viśvarūpa, who was most powerful, composed a protective prayer known as the Nārāyaṇa-kavaca. By this intelligent mantra, he took away the opulence of the demons and gave it to Mahendra, the King of heaven.

verses.sanskrit: यया गुप्त: सहस्राक्षो जिग्येऽसुरचमूर्विभु: ।तां प्राह स महेन्द्राय विश्वरूप उदारधी: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: yayā guptaḥ sahasrākṣojigye ’sura-camūr vibhuḥtāṁ prāha sa mahendrāyaviśvarūpa udāra-dhīḥ
verses.text: Viśvarūpa, who was most liberal, spoke to King Indra [Sahasrākṣa] the secret hymn that protected Indra and conquered the military power of the demons.
